reason: FALSE constant-universality claim at the chosen eta_0. Node 1.8(a) asserts 'the eta_0<1/4 of GT-Pprops is universal. Node 1.7 sets C=max(C_1,C_2,C_3,C_4) and eta_0=1/4, both universal and independent of dim H.' The value eta_0=1/4 is INVALID and breaks the very universality 1.8 is supposed to certify. Two coupled defects: (1) CONTRACT VIOLATION: GT-Pprops (=lem-P-properties, root contract byte: 'For eta <= eta_0 < 1/4 ...') requires eta <= eta_0 with eta_0 STRICTLY < 1/4. With the closed interval eta in [0,eta_0] and eta_0=1/4 (node 1.7's own wording 'for eta in [0,eta_0]'), the top of the range eta=1/4 violates eta < 1/4, so P=theta(2Phi-1) is not even guaranteed idempotent there and the whole near-fixed-algebra setup (Im P) is undefined. (2) DIVERGENT CONSTANT: every 'easy' constant C_1,C_2,C_3 is built from the single GT-Pprops constant C=4*C(eta_0) with C(eta_0)=sum_{n>=1}|C(-1/2,n)|(4 eta_0)^{n-1} (see lem-P-properties node 1.2: 'C(eta_0):=sum_{n>=1}|a_n|(4 eta_0)^{n-1} < infinity SINCE 4 eta_0 < rho=1'). This sum is finite ONLY for 4 eta_0 < 1, i.e. eta_0 < 1/4. At eta_0=1/4 one has 4 eta_0=1=rho and sum_{n>=1}|C(-1/2,n)| t^{n-1} diverges as t->1 (the tail of (1-t)^(-1/2)-1 over t is unbounded; cf. lem-P-properties challenge ch-e0d1faba: ratio ((1-t)^(-1/2)-1)/t ~ 0.83 at t=1/2, ~9.1 at t=0.99, -> infinity). Hence at eta_0=1/4 the constant C=C_1=C_2=C_3 is INFINITE -- not a universal finite constant -- so JB1-JB3 carry no finite eps and the eps-JB conclusion fails. CORRECT statement: eta_0 must be any FIXED value STRICTLY < 1/4 (e.g. eta_0=1/8), at which C(eta_0) is finite and dimension-free; node 1.7's 'eta_0<=1' constraint is then non-binding and the binding constraint is eta_0 < 1/4 (strict), NOT eta_0=1/4. Part (b) (no complete positivity) is sound and not challenged; the defect is solely the eta_0=1/4 endorsement and the resulting false universal-constant claim in part (a).
